title: Validate Palindromes with Case and Spaces

statement.markdown: Implement a function that takes a string as input and returns True if a string is a palindrome, ignoring non-alphanumeric characters and case. Otherwise, return False.
statement.inputFormat: A non-empty string with a mix of characters, digits, and spaces.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the input string is a palindrome.
statement.notes: Pay attention to preserving the original character case in the output.

examples.input: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama
examples.explanation: The given string is a palindrome when ignoring non-alphanumeric characters and case.
The given string is not a palindrome when ignoring non-alphanumeric characters and case.

hints.text: Consider using two pointers to traverse the string from both ends.

editorial.title: Editorial: Validating Palindromes
editorial.intuition: The key idea is to create a new string that contains only alphanumeric characters and ignore case.
editorial.bruteForce: Iterate over the entire string, and for each character, check if it is alphanumeric. If it's not, skip it.
editorial.betterSolution: Use two pointers to traverse the string from both ends.
editorial.optimalSolution: Create a helper function to remove non-alphanumeric characters and convert the string to lowercase.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution correctly identifies palindromes by correctly ignoring non-alphanumeric characters and case.
editorial.dryRun: Run the optimal solution on a few examples to verify its correctness.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public:void solve(string s) { // TODO: implement } };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public:void solve(String s) { // TODO: implement } };
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, s: str) -> None: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public:void solve(string s) { bool isPalindrome = true; for (int i = 0, j = s.length() - 1; i <= j; i++, j--) { while (i < j && !isalnum(s[i])) i++; while (i < j && !isalnum(s[j])) j--; if (tolower(s[i]) != tolower(s[j])) { isPalindrome = false; break; } } std::cout << (isPalindrome ? "True" : "False") << std::endl; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public:void solve(String s) { boolean isPalindrome = true; for (int i = 0, j = s.length() - 1; i <= j; i++, j--) { while (i < j && !Character.isLetterOrDigit(s.charAt(i))) i++; while (i < j && !Character.isLetterOrDigit(s.charAt(j))) j--; if (Character.toLowerCase(s.charAt(i)) != Character.toLowerCase(s.charAt(j))) { isPalindrome = false; break; } } System.out.println(isPalindrome); } };
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var solve = function(s) { var isPalindrome = true; for (var i = s.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) { while (i >= 0 && !isalnum(s[i])) i--; while (i >= 0 && !isalnum(s[s.length - 1 - i])) s.length - 1 - i--; if (tolower(s[i]) != tolower(s[s.length - 1 - i])) { isPalindrome = false; break; } } console.log(isPalindrome); };

testCases.input: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama
